_meta.title: wiTECH UDS Function Map
_meta.description: Machine-readable mapping of wiTECH Erlang helper functions to UDS service IDs, sub-functions, and DIDs. Derived from CDA SWF constant-pool trace, ISO 14229-1, and SRT Lab live implementation. Entries marked dump_verified:false need confirmation against wiTECH_wde.DMP once available.

enter_diagnostic_session.notes: wiTECH uses 0x03 (extended) before any security-access. 0x01=default, 0x02=programming also used in flash flows.

pre_unlock_init.notes: Alias for enter_diagnostic_session(0x03). Must be called before read_seed / send_key — the SGW (and most FCA ECUs) will NRC 0x22 a 27 01 in default session.

read_seed.notes: SGW returns a 4-byte seed. Key is derived via XTEA-32 (local path) or forwarded to manufacturing server (wiTECH subscription path).

send_key.notes: 4-byte key payload for local XTEA path. Server-signed cert path may use a longer payload (8–16 bytes — dump_verified:false). NRC 0x35 = invalidKey, NRC 0x36 = exceededNumberOfAttempts.

send_tester_present.notes: wiTECH sends with suppressPosRspMsgIndicationBit (0x80) so the ECU does not respond, minimising bus traffic during keep-alive. Without this the session expires after ~5 s.

disable_normal_messages.notes: Third byte is communicationType: 0x01 = normalCommunicationMessages. Called before flash download to silence normal bus traffic. Must be paired with enable_normal_messages after flash exit.

enable_normal_messages.notes: Restores normal bus traffic after flash session. Third byte is communicationType: 0x01.

disable_fault_setting.notes: Stops the ECU from setting new DTCs during a flash or write session. Should be called before programming and re-enabled after.

enable_fault_setting.notes: Re-enables DTC setting after a programming session. Pair with enable_normal_messages and ECUReset.

read_memory.frameExample: 23 14 AA AA AA AA LL LL
read_memory.notes: addressAndLengthFormatIdentifier 0x14 = 1-byte length, 4-byte address. FCA ECUs typically use 4-byte address + 2-byte length (0x24). Requires extended session + security access unlock first.

write_memory.frameExample: 3D 14 AA AA AA AA LL LL <data>
write_memory.notes: Direct memory write by address. Less common than WriteDataByIdentifier (0x2E) for EEPROM writes on FCA ECUs — many modules gate EEPROM writes through DIDs. Requires unlock.

read_vin.notes: Returns the 17-character ASCII VIN stored in the module. Available in default session on most FCA ECUs (no security access required for read).

read_partnumber.notes: OEM part number string (typically 10–14 ASCII chars on FCA). Available in default session.

read_flash_partnumber.notes: Flash/assembly part number. Some FCA modules use 0xF188 instead — [DUMP-TBD] to confirm which DID wiTECH's read_flash_partnumber function actually addresses.

read_software_number.notes: Software version / calibration identifier. Available in default session.

read_hardware_number.notes: Hardware version number. Some FCA modules use 0xF193 (SystemSupplierECUHardwareVersionNumber) — [DUMP-TBD] to confirm.

veh_sgw.summary: Vehicle SGW authentication dispatcher. Routes to local XTEA path or manufacturing-server cert path based on wiTECH subscription state.

veh_unlock.summary: Top-level vehicle unlock sequence. Enters extended diagnostic session, calls veh_sgw, then permits downstream module writes through the SGW.

device_unlock_ecu.summary: Per-ECU security-access unlock. Runs 10 03 + 27 01/02 on a specified CAN ID pair. Wraps read_seed + send_key for non-SGW modules.

whs_ecu_unlock.summary: Warehouse/workshop ECU unlock. Variant of device_unlock_ecu used in bench programming flows where the vehicle harness is absent.

whs_ecu_memory.summary: Warehouse ECU memory read/write. Wraps read_memory (0x23) and write_memory (0x3D) with address/length validation.

whs_flash.summary: Flash programming session manager. Enters programming session (10 02), gates the SGW, silences normal traffic, transfers data via 34/36/37, exits and resets.

whs_ecu_raw.summary: Raw UDS passthrough. Sends arbitrary byte arrays to a CAN ID pair without service-layer validation. Used for development and manual diagnostics.

flash_sup.summary: Flash supervisor. Manages block checksums, retry logic, and erase confirmation during a flash session. Called by whs_flash and vrflash.

jcanflash.summary: J1939/CAN flash. Flash programming over J1939 transport, used for heavy-duty variant modules or truck gateway ECUs.

rmflash.summary: ROM-mode flash. Programs modules that respond to ROM boot commands rather than standard UDS — vendor-specific bootstrap protocol.

vrflash.summary: Vehicle Reflash orchestrator. Calls whs_flash per-module and manages the overall multi-ECU reflash sequence including dependency ordering.

protocol_kline.summary: K-line transport handler (ISO 9141-2 / ISO 14230 KWP2000). Used for pre-CAN modules on pre-2005 Chrysler platforms.

protocol_services.summary: UDS service dispatcher. Routes incoming service IDs to the correct Erlang handler — effectively the UDS server loop for the wiTECH BEAM node.

local_xtea_path.description: On-device XTEA seed/key computation. Does not require network access.
local_xtea_path.algorithm: XTEA-32 ECB, key = K_SGW, v0=seed, v1=~seed, return c0 (high u32)

server_signed_path.description: Manufacturing-server cert path used by wiTECH with active subscription. The tool forwards the seed to a Stellantis HTTPS endpoint and gets a signed cert back.

sequence.description: request_sgw_cert: signed certificate for this seed
Send signed cert as key payload

server_signed_path.notes: CDA symbols: SGWJsonHTTPAction, SecurityGatewayOfflineDongleServiceAPIVersionCommand, SecurityGatewayResult. Offline dongle variant caches the cert locally (dongleUnlockSecurityGateway).
